name: Buttery Drop Cookies and Variations
recipeInstructions:
Preparation Time: 20 Minutes Bake Time: 7 to 9 Minutes
1. Heat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease baking sheets with melted Butter Flavor Crisco.
2. In a medium bowl, cream together the 1/2 cup melted Butter Flavor Crisco, sugar, and milk using an electric mixer at medium speed until well blended.
3. Beat in the egg and vanilla extract.
4. Sift together the flour, salt, and baking powder; add to the creamed mixture alternately with the egg mixture, mixing only until combined.
5. Drop rounded tablespoonfuls of dough about 2 inches apart onto prepared baking sheets.
6. Bake for 7 to 9 minutes or until lightly browned. Brush the tops of warm cookies with additional melted Butter Flavor Crisco for a more buttery flavor. Transfer to a cooling rack.
Makes approximately 3 dozen 2-inch cookies.
Note: For larger 3-inch cookies, drop two rounded tablespoonfuls of dough into each mound and space them about 3 inches apart. Bake at 375°F (190°C) for 11 to 13 minutes.
Variations:
1. Frosted Buttery Drop Cookies
- Bake and cool the 2-inch butter cookies.
- Frost with a creamy vanilla frosting after cooling.
Creamy Vanilla Frosting:
Combine melted Butter Flavor Crisco, confectioners' sugar, milk, and vanilla in a bowl at low speed until smooth and creamy.
2. Chocolate Dipped Cookies
- Bake and cool the 2-inch butter cookies.
- Melt 1 cup semi-sweet chocolate with melted Butter Flavor Crisco on very low heat or microwave power.
- Dip one end of each cooled cookie into the chocolate, then place on waxed paper until firm.
3. Raspberry Coconut Cookies
- Bake and cool the cookies.
- Spread raspberry jam onto each hot cookie and sprinkle with flake coconut before cooling.
4. Chocolate Sandwich Cookies
- Bake and cool the 2-inch butter cookies.
- Spread melted chocolate filling onto half of the cooled cookies, then top with remaining cookies. Gently press together to form sandwiches.
Chocolate Filling:
Melt Butter Flavor Crisco in a saucepan over low heat. Remove from heat and stir in cocoa powder and salt. Add milk and vanilla; blend until smooth. Gradually add confectioners' sugar, mixing until the desired spreading consistency is reached.
